clean_text: Hurricane Rum Cocktail Recipe | How to make a Hurricane Cocktail | BACARDÍ Global OUR RUMS OUR RUMS White Rum BACARDÍ CARTA BLANCA Flavors Flavors Range Spiced BACARDÍ Spiced Dark Rum BACARDÍ Carta Oro BACARDÍ Carta Negra BACARDÍ Añejo Cuatro BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Diez BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Limitada ready to enjoy BACARDÍ x coca-cola Ready to Drink cocktails Ready to Serve cocktails BACARDÍ Coquito View all our rums Cocktails Experiences Experiences Casa BACARDÍ Puerto Rico Visit the World’s largest premium rum distillery. SUSTAINABILITY Our World FAQS BASE RUM BACARDÍ Carta Negra rum COCKTAILS Hurricane Big in New Orleans and served to this day in the French quarter, the Hurricane is truly the drink of Mardi Gras because of its exotic blend of passion fruit, orange, and a hint of lime. Enjoy the exotic blend of passionfruit, orange, and a hint of lime. How to make a Hurricane 50 ml BACARDÍ Carta Negra rum 37.5 ml BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum 50 ml orange juice 25 ml lime juice 25 ml passionfruit syrup 1 tsp grenadine syrup Servings Copy Hurricane ingredients Copied! Hurricane Recipe One step at a time 1 Combine Combine all ingredients in a shaker with plenty of ice. 2 Shake Shake vigorously. 3 Strain Strain into a hurricane glass filled with ice. 4 Garnish Garnish with a lemon wheel, orange peel and a cherry. MERCHANDISE GET KITTED OUT Glasses. Squeezers. Cocktail kits. Discover everything you need to create standout BACARDÍ cocktails. SHOP NOW OUR RECOMMENDED RUM COCKTAILS Like This? Try These If this is your kind of cocktail, here’s our selection of other BACARDÍ cocktails that might suit your taste View all Rum cocktails Rum Punch Zombie Cuatro Smash Lime Cooler Jungle Bird Drag to discover more View all Rum cocktails FAQ WANT TO KNOW MORE? How do you make rum? Rum is made using only three ingredients: molasses from sugarcane, yeast and water. Ingredients are fermented, then distilled, aged, filtered and blended. What is the difference between dark, gold and white rum? Rum is a wonderfully complex and diverse spirit. Color can give us clues about time spent aging or the flavours that might be found within. Dark can be bold, sweet (or dry) and even smoky, gold is rich and soothing, and white is smooth, subtle and often refreshing. But color can be misleading. The best way to truly understand a rum is to taste it for yourself. SOCIAL MOVE WITH US A soft sway, or a lively dance. We have something for everyone.
